title: Orchestrated control-plane swap and fallback playbook
preview:
Purpose and scope

This playbook defines a repeatable, low-risk process to orchestrate a control-plane swap between Redwood's hosted control plane and a customer-private control plane (or between two private control-plane versions) with explicit fallback and rollback steps. It covers: pre-swap readiness checks, phas...
text:
Purpose and scope

This playbook defines a repeatable, low-risk process to orchestrate a control-plane swap between Redwood's hosted control plane and a customer-private control plane (or between two private control-plane versions) with explicit fallback and rollback steps. It covers: pre-swap readiness checks, phased swap orchestration, KV cache handover, traffic steering, verification gates, automated rollback triggers, and post-swap evidence collection. This document is intended for operators, field engineers, and SREs responsible for private deployments.

When to use this playbook

- Planned upgrades to control-plane software where a full in-place upgrade is impractical (major version or architecture change).
- Emergency diversion from hosted control plane to private control plane (or vice-versa) for customer isolation or regulatory requirements.
- Migration between control-plane ...

text:
Overview
========

Purpose: This guide defines a single, repeatable process for performing controlled rollouts of Redwood Private deployments (VPC and on-prem) that enforces compatibility gates across runtime, model artifacts, packaging, and network/KMS dependencies. It collates pre-installation checks, staged rollout steps, day-2 validation, rollback criteria, and customer-facing readiness items so that field installs, upgrades, and hot-swap model rollouts are low-risk and observable.

Scope: applies to: 
- New environment installs (VPC private clusters and on-prem air-gapped installs)
- Versioned upgrades of the control plane and serving runtime
- Model binary swaps that change kernel/quantization profiles or KV cache format

Owners and stakeholders
-----------------------
- Primary owner: eng-private-deployments (Deployment Engineering)
- Runbook owner: on-call Private Deploys SRE ...

Overview

This document describes recommended networking bridge and fallback patterns for Redwood Private (VPC and on‑prem) deployments. It focuses on field-safe connectivity designs that preserve control‑plane reachability, allow safe model hot‑swaps, and provide deterministic failure modes when network paths are constrained (air‑gapped customers, intermittent interconnects, or restricted egress environments).

- Field operations and SRE teams responsible for day‑2 networking
- Solutions engineering and customer success for pre‑sales readiness checks

Scope and goals

- Provide a small set of vetted bridge and fallback patterns that balance security, observability, and reliability.
- Document rollout and rollback procedures for network modifications without requiring full service restarts.

title: Latency-Optimized Local Model Fanout Architecture
preview:
Overview

This page documents the Latency-Optimized Local Model Fanout Architecture (LOLMFA) for Redwood Private deployments. The pattern is intended for customers who require sub-50ms median (p50) inference latency for common short requests while preserving the ability to route heavier or higher‑quality requests to...
text:
Overview

This page documents the Latency-Optimized Local Model Fanout Architecture (LOLMFA) for Redwood Private deployments. The pattern is intended for customers who require sub-50ms median (p50) inference latency for common short requests while preserving the ability to route heavier or higher‑quality requests to central GPU pools.

Goals

- Provide a predictable low-latency path for small/short-text workloads (p50 < 50ms, p95 < 120ms) using local, quantized model shards.
- Retain correctness and quality by transparently fanouting requests to central inference for long-context or compute‑intensive queries.
- Ensure graceful degradation and clear rollback/upgrade paths for both local and central tiers.
- Minimize customer network egress and meet privacy constraints (models and tokens may remain within customer VPC).

High-level topology

1) Edge nodes (customer-managed in VPC or on-...

title: Tenant Traffic Forensics & Snapshot Bridge Reference Architecture
preview:
Overview:
This reference architecture describes the Tenant Traffic Forensics & Snapshot Bridge: a secure, operator-friendly pattern for capturing tenant traffic traces, KV-cache snapshots, and control-plane metadata from private VPC and on-prem Redwood Private deployments for post-incident analysis, regulatory audit...
text:
Overview:
This reference architecture describes the Tenant Traffic Forensics & Snapshot Bridge: a secure, operator-friendly pattern for capturing tenant traffic traces, KV-cache snapshots, and control-plane metadata from private VPC and on-prem Redwood Private deployments for post-incident analysis, regulatory audits, and model-drift investigations. The design balances minimal performance impact, strong chain-of-custody guarantees, and customer privacy controls.

Goals:
- Provide an immutable, auditable snapshot pipeline for tenant state and request traces.
- Ensure snapshots can be collected with <=5% added tail-latency under normal load via asynchronous capture.
- Maintain tenant data isolation: all forensic snapshots are encrypted with customer KMS/HSM keys where required.
- Offer deterministic retention and evidence packaging for audits (customer-facing artifact).

When to use thi...

Overview

This playbook documents a standardized Tenant Performance QA and Hotpatch Validation Protocol for private (VPC/on‑prem) deployments. It is intended for operators, release engineers, and SREs responsible for applying critical runtime patches, microcode/model hotpatches, or small config changes that must be validated in production-like conditions with minimal customer impact.

- Ensure regressions in latency, throughput, and correctness are detected within SLO windows.
- Provide deterministic rollback/runforward criteria and evidence collection for post‑change audits.

Scope and applicability

- Applies to: runtime binary hotpatches, kernel/driver micro-updates (where supported), runtime config changes (batching, cache TTLs), model binary swaps that do not require full cold restart, and small orchestration/placement fixes.

-------

- Not in scope: cloud-hosted Redwood managed API upgrades (separate process).

Overview

This document defines the Operator Safety Contract for performing regulation-sensitive cutovers (major upgrades, tenant-facing model swaps, and region-to-region data sovereignty moves) in private deployments (VPC and on-prem). It combines a contractual checklist (what operators must guarantee), a step-by-step playbook for cutovers, a rollback/rollforward decision tree, and an evidence-preservation telemetry contract suitable for audits.

Scope

- Applies to all private deployment cutovers that: change runtime model weights or quantization profiles, alter KV cache migrations, rotate tenant KMS boundaries, modify network egress policies, or change tenant-region assignments.
- Intended audience: Field Operators, NOC, Platform SRE, Security/Compliance owners, and Customer Technical Contact.
- Not for trivial config flips (those are covered by the standard operator runbook).

Summary:

This playbook defines operator procedures and hardened configuration recommendations to detect, contain, and recover from VPC-level failure domains in multitenant private deployments. It is intended for Redwood operators, field engineers, and SREs responsible for private VPC and on-prem tenant fleets.

Goals:

- Preserve tenant data integrity (KV caches, session state) and avoid cascading model-swap regressions.

Scope and audience:
- Scope: VPC-sited control/data plane failures that affect one or more tenants in a private deployment. Includes cross-VPC routing incidents, NAT/gateway failures, and service-subn...

title: Field Operator Offline Debugging & Synthetic SLA Replay Playbook
preview:

Use this when a customer site experiences intermittent or reproducible SLA regressions but live t...
text:
Overview:

This playbook describes the standardized process for field operators and on-call engineers to perform offline debugging of private-deploy runtime incidents and to run deterministic synthetic SLA replay tests. Use this when a customer site experiences intermittent or reproducible SLA regressions but live traffic replay is not possible or desirable (air-gapped sites, PII constraints, or when reproducing production load would risk further degradation).

Goals:
- Produce a deterministic reproduction of the failing condition with minimal customer impact.
- Validate mitigation and rollout changes by running synthetic SLA replays against a controlled replica of the tenant environment.
- Capture forensics and telemetry artifacts required for post-incident review and compliance evidence.

When to use this playbook:
- Repeated elevated p95/p99 latency for inference endpoints with no ...

title: Private deployments: optimization config version pinning and rollback (Helm + installer)
preview:

The Private deployment supports the same core semanti...
text:
## Overview
This guide describes how **Private** (VPC/on-prem) operators manage **Optimization Config** versions (batching, caching, quantization profile selection) and execute a safe **rollback** when a change causes latency, error-rate, or quality regressions.

The Private deployment supports the same core semantics as Hosted/Dedicated:
- Immutable **Optimization Config versions** stored in the **private control plane**.
- A single **active version pointer** (per scope) consumed by runtime workers.
- Rollout state tracking + audit events.

This page focuses on operator workflows that do not rely on Redwood-managed infrastructure: **Helm** and the **Private installer (redwood-private-installer / redwoodctl)**.

> Safety note: Rollback is designed to be low-risk and idempotent, but it is not “free.” Some changes (notably cache strategy or quantization profile changes) can cause brief ...

title: Private upgrades: rollback loop (RRB-17) Detection, workaround, and fix
preview:
# Overview
This page documents the "rollback loop" failure mode in Redwood Private upgrades tracked as **RRB-17** (also described as "stuck rollback" or "rollback keeps re-triggering").

This is customer-impacting because the installer can retry rollbacks indefinitely, leaving the cluster in a non-converged state.

...
text:
# Overview
This page documents the "rollback loop" failure mode in Redwood Private upgrades tracked as **RRB-17** (also described as "stuck rollback" or "rollback keeps re-triggering").

This is customer-impacting because the installer can retry rollbacks indefinitely, leaving the cluster in a non-converged state.

# Symptoms
- Console/installer status shows **Rollback in progress** for >30 minutes
- Installer controller logs repeat messages like "scheduling rollback retry"
- No forward progress after multiple rollback attempts

# Affected versions
- Observed in: 2.9.0 upgrades from 2.8.0
- Potentially affects other upgrade paths when CRD migration partially applies

# Detection
## Log indicators
Search installer-controller logs for repeated sequences:
- `status=FAILED`
- `scheduling rollback retry`
- references to `installer-rollback-lock`

## Operational threshold
Treat as rollback ...

title: Air-gapped installation: verify offline bundle signatures (Redwood Private)
preview:
## Purpose
This guide is used by **Solutions Engineering** and **Support** to help customers perform an **air-gapped (offline)** install or upgrade of **Redwood Private** using an **offline bundle** that is **cryptographically signed by Redwood**.

It provides:
- Step-by-step **preflight signature verification** com...
text:
## Purpose
This guide is used by **Solutions Engineering** and **Support** to help customers perform an **air-gapped (offline)** install or upgrade of **Redwood Private** using an **offline bundle** that is **cryptographically signed by Redwood**.

It provides:
- Step-by-step **preflight signature verification** commands
- Expected outputs and what “good” looks like
- Common failure modes and remediation
- What to collect for escalation

## Audience
- Solutions Engineers (SE/PS) supporting on-prem/VPC-private installs
- Support engineers triaging install failures

## What “signed bundle verification” protects
Verification blocks installs when:
- The bundle **manifest is modified** (tampering)
- Any referenced artifact hash **does not match** what was signed
- The signing key is **unknown/untrusted**
- The bundle attempts an unsupported **downgrade** (when downgrade protection is enabl...

title: Private Deployments: Regional Failover Guidance (Day-2 Ops)
preview:
## Purpose
This page provides **operational guidance** for implementing and running **regional failover** for **Redwood Private** deployments (VPC and on-prem). It focuses on:
- Supported failover patterns and which party owns what (**customer-managed vs Redwood-assisted**).

text:
## Purpose
This page provides **operational guidance** for implementing and running **regional failover** for **Redwood Private** deployments (VPC and on-prem). It focuses on:
- Supported failover patterns and which party owns what (**customer-managed vs Redwood-assisted**).
- Prerequisites and design constraints (capacity, data residency, network reachability, and control plane dependencies).
- Day-2 operational caveats (testing, observability, change management, and incident coordination).

This guidance is intentionally conservative: a “works on paper” failover that has not been drilled can increase customer impact during real incidents.

## Scope and non-goals
### In scope
- Private deployments running Redwood’s serving and/or control-plane components in customer-managed environments.
- Multi-region routing patterns (DNS, L7 proxy, anycast/edge steering) and operational playbooks....

text:
Overview

This playbook defines the field acceptance and safety certification criteria for offline / air‑gapped private deployments of the Redwood runtime. It is intended for delivery engineers, customer site operators, and SREs who perform the final acceptance verification and produce evidence for compliance packages.

Scope

- Applies to: on‑prem or isolated VPC deployments where control‑plane connectivity is intentionally restricted (air‑gapped or dark site).
- Excludes: hosted Redwood API, fully connected Dedicated deployments.
- Goal: provide a deterministic, repeatable test suite and sign‑off template to ensure the deployment meets safety, availability, and traceability requirements prior to customer handover.

Quick summary of acceptance outcomes (must all PASS to sign off)

1) Functional health: all core services report healthy and APIs respond to local queries.
2) Safety resi...

title: Offline bundle upgrade compatibility matrix (Redwood Private)
preview:
## Overview
This page defines the supported **upgrade** and **rollback** paths for **Redwood Private offline bundles** and how these paths interact with:
- **Signature verification** (fail-closed by default)
- **Trust store / key rotation** (multiple trusted public keys)
- **Downgrade prevention** (signed version me...
text:
## Overview
This page defines the supported **upgrade** and **rollback** paths for **Redwood Private offline bundles** and how these paths interact with:
- **Signature verification** (fail-closed by default)
- **Trust store / key rotation** (multiple trusted public keys)
- **Downgrade prevention** (signed version metadata + local install state)

This matrix is used by Solutions Engineering, Support, and SRE to qualify upgrade requests in air-gapped environments and to standardize rollback guidance.

### Definitions (terms used on this page)
- **Offline bundle**: A tar/zip bundle that contains charts/manifests, container image references and/or image tarballs, runtime/model artifacts, and a **canonical manifest** with hashes.
- **Signed manifest**: The canonical manifest plus a signature envelope (detached `manifest.sig` or equivalent) produced by Redwood Release Engineering.
- **Insta...

title: Control-plane backup CronJob RBAC preflight (private clusters)
preview:
## Overview
This page defines the RBAC preflight checks for the control-plane backup CronJob deployed via the private installer Helm chart ("redwood-private"). It is intended to prevent and quickly diagnose the most common failure mode: backup Jobs failing immediately with Kubernetes RBAC "forbidden" errors when the...
text:
## Overview
This page defines the RBAC preflight checks for the control-plane backup CronJob deployed via the private installer Helm chart ("redwood-private"). It is intended to prevent and quickly diagnose the most common failure mode: backup Jobs failing immediately with Kubernetes RBAC "forbidden" errors when the backup CLI inventories cluster state.

This guidance supersedes earlier staging-only fixes that required manually patching Roles/RoleBindings. As of the current chart defaults, RBAC objects are created automatically when backups are enabled, but clusters with custom values or older chart versions may still drift.

## Scope
- Applies to: private control-plane deployments (staging and production)
- Namespace: typically `redwood-private`
- Workload: `CronJob/<release>-backup` (name varies by release)
- ServiceAccount: chart default is now `redwood-private-backup` (older relea...

title: Runbook: Offline bundle signature/manifest verification failures (Redwood Private)
preview:

This runbook is used by SRE/Support/PS to diagnose and resolve **offline bundle verification failures** for Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem), especially in **air-gapped** environments.

Verification covers:
- Bundle manifest integrity (hashes)
- Manifest signature validity (trusted key, signature matches man...
text:
## Purpose
This runbook is used by SRE/Support/PS to diagnose and resolve **offline bundle verification failures** for Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem), especially in **air-gapped** environments.

Verification covers:
- Bundle manifest integrity (hashes)
- Manifest signature validity (trusted key, signature matches manifest)
- Certificate validity window checks (not-yet-valid / expired)
- Downgrade/replay protection (signed version + local install state)

**Safety principle:** installer verification is designed to **fail closed**. Do not bypass verification except via an approved break-glass process.

---

## Symptoms (what you will see)
Typical user-facing errors during `preflight` / `install` include:
- **HASH_MISMATCH**: One or more artifacts do not match the manifest digest.
- **MANIFEST_PARSE_ERROR**: Manifest cannot be parsed or canonicalization mismatch.
- **SIGNATURE_INVALID**: S...

title: Air-gapped customer requirements summary (Redwood Private offline bundles)
preview:
## Purpose
This page curates common requirements observed across regulated and air-gapped Redwood Private customers (defense, public sector, healthcare, regulated banking, and industrial on-prem). The intent is to help Product/Eng/Solutions preempt security review questions and make consistent design tradeoffs for o...
text:
## Purpose
This page curates common requirements observed across regulated and air-gapped Redwood Private customers (defense, public sector, healthcare, regulated banking, and industrial on-prem). The intent is to help Product/Eng/Solutions preempt security review questions and make consistent design tradeoffs for offline bundles and signature verification.

This is a synthesis of repeated patterns from discovery calls, pilots, and security questionnaires. It is not a contract; treat as a requirements library.

## Scope
Covers:
- Allowed cryptography and signature formats
- Key custody / HSM expectations
- Offline time sources (time skew, validity windows)
- Artifact transport constraints (media, size, integrity)
- Approval processes and evidence artifacts expected for installs/upgrades

Out of scope:
- Customer-signed bundles (delegated signing) as a GA requirement (tracked separatel...

title: Offline bundle signing + verification design (Redwood Private on-prem)
preview:
## Overview
This document defines the engineering design for cryptographically signing Redwood Private offline bundles and verifying them during air-gapped installation and upgrade.

We are solving two problems:
1) **Integrity + provenance** for offline artifacts (bundle content cannot be modified without detection)...
text:
## Overview
This document defines the engineering design for cryptographically signing Redwood Private offline bundles and verifying them during air-gapped installation and upgrade.

We are solving two problems:
1) **Integrity + provenance** for offline artifacts (bundle content cannot be modified without detection).
2) **Safe operational UX** in air-gapped environments (clear failures, predictable rotation, break-glass path).

This design intentionally piggybacks on the existing on-prem/offline bundle installer architecture. It does not redesign packaging, registry mirroring, or Helm chart structure.

---

## Goals
- Sign offline bundles in the Redwood release pipeline using protected keys and auditable steps.
- Verify signatures and artifact hashes during installer preflight, failing closed by default.
- Support key rotation without bricking offline customers (multi-key trust store)...

title: Guide: configure customer-managed audit log export (Private deployments)
preview:
# Summary
This guide explains how to enable **customer-managed audit log export** for **Redwood Private** deployments (VPC and on-prem). In Private mode, the customer typically operates the cluster and networking, and Redwood provides the export connector as part of the platform bundle.

# Preconditions
- Customer h...
text:
# Summary
This guide explains how to enable **customer-managed audit log export** for **Redwood Private** deployments (VPC and on-prem). In Private mode, the customer typically operates the cluster and networking, and Redwood provides the export connector as part of the platform bundle.

# Preconditions
- Customer has deployed Redwood Private version that includes `audit-export-connector` (Private bundle v2.8.0+).
- Customer has created a destination bucket/container and configured encryption and retention.
- Security controls are reviewed and accepted by the customer per contract/security questionnaire.

# Supported sinks
- AWS S3
- Google Cloud Storage
- Azure Blob Storage

# Required inputs
Collect the following before changes:
- `sink`: s3|gcs|azure
- `region`: destination region
- `destination`: bucket/container name
- `prefix`: path prefix for objects
- `auth`: workload identity...

text:
## Overview
This page defines the **offline bundle** file format used to install or upgrade **Redwood Private** in **air-gapped / offline** environments. It specifies:
- Bundle layout (directory structure + required artifacts)
- The **bundle manifest** (schema + hashing requirements)
- SBOM inclusion/pointers
- Compatibility rules (forward/backward)
- Size and packaging constraints for common offline transport workflows

This page is the source of truth for what the installer expects on disk. Signing and verification behavior is defined in the design doc and ADR (linked above).

---

## Goals
1. Enable offline install/upgrade with **no network fetches** (installer must operate fully from the bundle).
2. Provide a deterministic, machine-verifiable inventory of contents (hashes + versions).
3. Support cryptographic signing of the manifest (integrity + provenance).
4. Support incremental...

title: Private Deployment: Customer KMS Integration Operator Guide
preview:
## Overview
This guide explains how to configure **customer-managed key management** for Redwood Inference **Private** deployments using AWS KMS, Google Cloud KMS, or Azure Key Vault. It is intended for operators responsible for install/upgrade, key permissions, validation, and incident response.

Redwood Private us...
text:
## Overview
This guide explains how to configure **customer-managed key management** for Redwood Inference **Private** deployments using AWS KMS, Google Cloud KMS, or Azure Key Vault. It is intended for operators responsible for install/upgrade, key permissions, validation, and incident response.

Redwood Private uses **envelope encryption**:
- A **Key Encryption Key (KEK)** is hosted in the customer’s KMS/HSM-backed service.
- Redwood generates and stores **Data Encryption Keys (DEKs)** (encrypted/wrapped by the KEK) for protecting sensitive material at rest (e.g., secrets/config artifacts, control-plane state that requires encryption).
- Runtime decrypt operations call into the customer KMS via the standardized Redwood KMS client wrapper (timeouts/retries/circuit breaker; see ADR-007).

This guide focuses on:
- Required identities and permissions (least privilege)
- Network connecti...

text:
## Purpose
Private deployments (VPC and on-prem) have different constraints than Hosted/Dedicated: upgrades are customer-coordinated, frequently air-gapped, and often require formal change-management evidence. This page defines how Redwood-generated model release notes map to **Private upgrade bundles**, how to package release notes for **offline environments**, and what **rollback documentation** must accompany each bundle.

This guidance applies to:
- Redwood Private control plane + runtime upgrades
- Model catalog updates shipped as offline artifacts (weights + metadata)
- Customer environments requiring change tickets, audit trails, and pre-approved rollbacks

## Definitions
- **Upgrade bundle**: A versioned artifact delivered to a Private customer containing runtime components (images/charts), optional model payloads, metadata, and release notes.
- **Bundle release notes**: The c...

text:
## Purpose
This checklist is the required go/no-go gate for upgrading an *enterprise* Redwood Private deployment (VPC or on-prem). It defines the minimum validations, evidence, and sign-offs required before starting the maintenance window, and the stop/rollback criteria during execution.

## Scope
Applies to any customer production Private environment upgrade of: (a) control plane components (operator, controller, API gateway), (b) serving runtime/data plane images, (c) cluster dependencies shipped by Redwood (ingress, cert-manager bundles, logging agents), or (d) installer/upgrade tooling.

> Policy dependencies: This page is the operational gate, but approvals/comms requirements are defined in the Change Management Policy and Planned Maintenance Comms SOP. Security-specific sign-off requirements are defined in Secure SDLC: Security Sign-off for Customer Deployments. SLO/error budget...

text:
## Purpose
This runbook defines the supported, low-risk procedure for upgrading the **Redwood Private VPC Terraform modules (alpha)** in customer or Redwood-managed AWS accounts, and how to rollback safely if an upgrade introduces networking regressions.

Scope:
- Applies to the `redwood-terraform` module suite used for **AWS VPC-based private deployments** (EKS assumed).
- Focuses on networking + foundational infra modules (VPC/subnets/routing/NAT, endpoints/DNS, security groups, flow logs/log sinks, IAM scaffolding).
- Applies to both **design partner pilots** and internal dogfood environments.

Non-goals:
- Full application/cluster rollback (handled by `redwood-private-installer` + Helm rollouts).
- Major topology migrations (e.g., CIDR changes, multi-VPC redesign). These require an engagement plan and change window.

## Ownership and escalation
Primary owners:
- Infra (modules + C...

text:
## Purpose
This runbook covers day-2 operations for the **Private Observability Minimum Pack** shipped with Redwood Private (VPC and on-prem). It is written for:
- Customer platform/SRE teams operating the private cluster
- Redwood Support and Redwood SRE during escalations

It focuses on:
- Post-install / post-upgrade verification (metrics, dashboards, alerts, logs)
- Common failure modes and fast diagnostics
- Safe remediation steps (including air-gapped constraints)
- Escalation paths and what data to collect

## What the “minimum pack” includes (v1)
- **Prometheus** scrape configuration for Redwood Private components + curated recording rules
- **Prometheus alert rules** aligned to Private SLO guidance (availability, latency burn, saturation/resource pressure)
- **Grafana dashboards** (runtime overview, control plane health, infra panels)
- **Log collection defaults** for control ...

title: Offline observability bundle packaging (air-gapped/on-prem) — Private minimum pack
preview:
## Overview
This page defines how Redwood packages and delivers the **Private observability minimum pack** for **offline / air-gapped on-prem** environments.

Goals:
- Ensure customers can install a supported baseline observability setup without outbound internet.
- Provide a deterministic, verifiable artifact bundl...
text:
## Overview
This page defines how Redwood packages and delivers the **Private observability minimum pack** for **offline / air-gapped on-prem** environments.

Goals:
- Ensure customers can install a supported baseline observability setup without outbound internet.
- Provide a deterministic, verifiable artifact bundle (images + dashboards + rules + configs) that can be mirrored into a customer registry.
- Make upgrades/rollbacks safe: versions are pinned, manifests are checksummed/signed, and compatibility constraints are documented.

Non-goals (v1):
- Customer-specific dashboard customization in the bundle.
- Long-retention or federation “premium” observability features.

## Audience
- Customer platform / infra teams operating Redwood Private in on-prem or restricted networks.
- Redwood Solutions Engineering and Support assisting air-gapped installs.
- Redwood Private Deployments engi...

title: Private Observability Minimum Pack (v1) — Overview
preview:
## Purpose
The **Private Observability Minimum Pack** is the baseline, supported observability bundle shipped with **Redwood Private** (VPC and on-prem) installs. It provides a standard set of:
- **Grafana dashboards** (health and performance for control plane + data plane)
- **Prometheus alert rules + recording rul...
text:
## Purpose
The **Private Observability Minimum Pack** is the baseline, supported observability bundle shipped with **Redwood Private** (VPC and on-prem) installs. It provides a standard set of:
- **Grafana dashboards** (health and performance for control plane + data plane)
- **Prometheus alert rules + recording rules** (golden signals + resource pressure)
- **Structured log collection defaults + export integrations** (sane defaults that work in private environments)

This pack is designed so that:
- Customer platform teams can monitor and operate Redwood Private day-2 without bespoke setup.
- Redwood Support/SRE can troubleshoot efficiently using a consistent set of signals.

## What this pack is (and is not)
### In scope (v1)
- Metrics for: control plane services, API gateway, policy/routing, serving runtime pods, installer/upgrade controller, and key integration points (e.g., model...

text:
## Overview
This guide walks through creating the AWS networking foundation for a Redwood Private deployment using the **Redwood VPC Terraform modules (alpha)**.

**What this guide covers (alpha scope):**
- AWS VPC + subnet layout (public + private)
- Routing and NAT defaults
- Required/recommended VPC endpoints and DNS behavior
- Logging defaults (VPC Flow Logs → CloudWatch Logs or S3)
- IAM scaffolding for the **installer execution role** (permission boundary-friendly)
- Validation steps before handing off to `redwood-private-installer`

**What this guide does not cover:**
- Installing EKS / Kubernetes (handled by your platform standards; Redwood supports EKS assumptions described in the reference architecture)
- Installing Redwood Private application components (handled by `redwood-private-installer` and Helm)
- Complex enterprise network topologies (Transit Gateway, shared service...

title: Alpha customers: upgrade notes for Private VPC Terraform modules (design partners)

text:
## Purpose
This page provides **alpha-specific upgrade guidance** for design partners using the **Redwood Private VPC Terraform modules (AWS)**. During alpha, we expect iterative changes to defaults, module interfaces, and validation behavior. This document explains:
- What changes are normal during alpha iterations (and what may be disruptive)
- How to **pin module versions** for stability
- How to **coordinate upgrade windows** with Redwood (SE + Eng)
- A practical **upgrade + rollback playbook** tailored to alpha

This page is intentionally conservative. If anything here conflicts with your organization’s change control, follow your internal process and use the coordination steps below.

---

## Audience
- Design partner **platform / infra engineers** applying Terraform to provision the Redwood Private VPC footprint
- **Security and networking teams** reviewing endpoint/DNS/logging...

title: AWS Private VPC Reference Architecture (EKS) — Redwood Private (Alpha)
preview:
## Purpose
This page describes Redwood’s reference AWS network footprint for **Redwood Private** deployments in a customer-owned AWS account, using **Amazon EKS** as the assumed Kubernetes substrate.

The intent is to:
- Reduce pilot risk by standardizing subnet layout, routing, endpoints, and logging defaults.

text:
## Purpose
This page describes Redwood’s reference AWS network footprint for **Redwood Private** deployments in a customer-owned AWS account, using **Amazon EKS** as the assumed Kubernetes substrate.

The intent is to:
- Reduce pilot risk by standardizing subnet layout, routing, endpoints, and logging defaults.
- Make installer + Helm behavior predictable across customers.
- Provide a baseline that is secure by default while still allowing controlled extension (VPN/DX, egress proxy, shared services).

This is the reference architecture implemented by the **VPC Terraform modules (alpha)**. If you deviate from this reference, ensure you capture the deltas in the pilot plan and validate installer prerequisites.

---

## Scope and assumptions
### In scope
- VPC/subnet strategy (3-AZ preferred)
- Routing/NAT strategy
- VPC endpoints (PrivateLink + gateway endpoints)
- Ingress/egress patter...

title: Redwood Private: Backup and Restore Strategy (Upgrade + Rollback)
preview:
## Overview
This document defines the backup and restore strategy used by **Redwood Private (VPC + on-prem/air-gapped)** during upgrades and rollbacks. The goal is to make upgrades **safe-by-default** by taking a deterministic, auditable backup prior to any irreversible step (notably DB schema migrations), and by pr...
text:
## Overview
This document defines the backup and restore strategy used by **Redwood Private (VPC + on-prem/air-gapped)** during upgrades and rollbacks. The goal is to make upgrades **safe-by-default** by taking a deterministic, auditable backup prior to any irreversible step (notably DB schema migrations), and by providing an operator-friendly restore path when rollback is required.

This strategy is implemented by the **redwood-private-installer** (backup orchestration + state persistence) and supported by Helm hook ordering (pre-upgrade jobs, timeouts, and rollback-safe behavior).

### Goals
- Ensure there is a **known-good restore point** prior to upgrade execution.
- Support both **VPC (cloud)** and **on-prem/air-gapped** deployments with consistent semantics.
- Provide multiple backup mechanisms to match storage backends: **EBS snapshots**, **CSI VolumeSnapshots**, and **logical ...

title: Runbook: Redwood Private upgrade + rollback (Day-2 Operations)
preview:
## Purpose
This runbook describes the operator procedure for upgrading **Redwood Private** (VPC and on-prem/air-gapped) using the **redwood-private-installer**. It covers:
- End-to-end upgrade flow (plan → prechecks → backups → apply → verify)
- What “upgrade success” means (verification gates)
- Common failure mode...
text:
## Purpose
This runbook describes the operator procedure for upgrading **Redwood Private** (VPC and on-prem/air-gapped) using the **redwood-private-installer**. It covers:
- End-to-end upgrade flow (plan → prechecks → backups → apply → verify)
- What “upgrade success” means (verification gates)
- Common failure modes and mitigations
- How to execute rollback safely and what rollback can/can’t guarantee

This doc is written for on-call SREs, customer operators, and Solutions Engineers assisting upgrades.

---

## Scope and guarantees
### Supported upgrade paths
- **Default supported path:** N-1 → N (single hop).
- Multi-hop (e.g., N-3 → N) is **not automated** in the initial release; follow Support guidance.
- Installer enforces a **version compatibility policy** and will block unsupported paths.

### Key invariants (do not bypass)
- **Prechecks must pass** for all blocking checks befo...

title: Rollback semantics and hooks (Redwood Private upgrades)
preview:
## Purpose
This page defines **rollback semantics** for Redwood Private upgrades and the **hook contracts** used by the installer and Helm charts to rollback safely and deterministically.

Goals:
- Provide clear **entry points** for rollback across phases (precheck → backup → control plane → data plane).
- Define a ...
text:
## Purpose
This page defines **rollback semantics** for Redwood Private upgrades and the **hook contracts** used by the installer and Helm charts to rollback safely and deterministically.

Goals:
- Provide clear **entry points** for rollback across phases (precheck → backup → control plane → data plane).
- Define a **hook interface** for components (control plane services, data plane workloads, storage/migrations) to participate in rollback.
- Make rollback **safe by default**, **idempotent**, and **auditable**.
- Document how to handle **partial upgrades**, **schema migrations**, and **model cache/state**.

Non-goals:
- Full blue/green cutover for every Private environment (see ADR).
- Guarantee rollback after every possible irreversible data migration (we require explicit “rollback supported” signaling per release).

---

## Definitions
- **Upgrade attempt**: one execution of an upg...

title: On‑prem upgrade bundle format (air‑gapped) — Redwood Private
preview:
## Purpose
Redwood Private on‑prem (including fully air‑gapped environments) upgrades are delivered as a **single, self‑contained upgrade bundle**. The bundle includes all artifacts required by the installer to validate, plan, and execute an upgrade **without reaching the public internet**.

This document defines:
-...
text:
## Purpose
Redwood Private on‑prem (including fully air‑gapped environments) upgrades are delivered as a **single, self‑contained upgrade bundle**. The bundle includes all artifacts required by the installer to validate, plan, and execute an upgrade **without reaching the public internet**.

This document defines:
- Bundle packaging requirements and directory layout
- The **manifest** (what it must contain and how it’s validated)
- Checksums and signature verification requirements
- Offline dependency handling (container images, Helm charts, CLIs)
- How `redwood-private-installer` locates, verifies, and uses bundles

## Terminology
- **Bundle**: A `.tar.zst` (preferred) or `.tar.gz` archive containing a manifest and all upgrade artifacts.
- **Artifact**: A file or OCI object required for upgrade (charts, images, binaries, migration jobs).
- **Mirror**: Customer-managed offline registr...

title: Redwood Private: Upgrade + Rollback Workflow (Overview)
preview:
## Purpose
This page describes the end-to-end, operator-facing upgrade workflow for **Redwood Private** deployments (VPC and on-prem/air-gapped). It is intended as a stable overview of:
- Upgrade phases and sequencing between **control plane** and **data plane**
- Safety invariants (what must be true before we proce...
text:
## Purpose
This page describes the end-to-end, operator-facing upgrade workflow for **Redwood Private** deployments (VPC and on-prem/air-gapped). It is intended as a stable overview of:
- Upgrade phases and sequencing between **control plane** and **data plane**
- Safety invariants (what must be true before we proceed)
- Required inputs and artifacts
- Operator UX (CLI-first, with optional Console visibility)
- Rollback entry points and guarantees

This is the canonical workflow referenced by the installer, Helm packaging, Terraform variables, and support/runbooks.

---

## Definitions
**Private Control Plane (CP)**
- Private management services (API, auth integration, policy, upgrade coordinator) running in the customer cluster/VPC or on-prem environment.

**Private Data Plane (DP)**
- Inference runtime components and worker pools that serve traffic.

**Installer**
- `redwood-private...

title: Private Upgrades: Preflight (Precheck) Specification
preview:
## Purpose
This page specifies **preflight checks ("prechecks")** executed by `redwood-private-installer` before any Redwood Private upgrade steps are performed. The goal is to prevent common upgrade failures by validating environment readiness up front and producing **actionable remediation guidance**.

This spec c...
text:
## Purpose
This page specifies **preflight checks ("prechecks")** executed by `redwood-private-installer` before any Redwood Private upgrade steps are performed. The goal is to prevent common upgrade failures by validating environment readiness up front and producing **actionable remediation guidance**.

This spec covers:
- Check categories and required inputs
- Pass/fail criteria and thresholds
- **Blocking vs warning** behavior
- Standardized error codes, messages, and operator remediation
- Special considerations for **VPC** vs **on-prem/air-gapped** deployments

Non-goals:
- Replacing runtime monitoring during upgrades (see SRE alert runbooks)
- Full coverage for every historical migration edge case (installer will explicitly identify "manual intervention required")

---

## Definitions
- **Private deployment**: Redwood Private control plane + data plane running in customer-contro...

title: Runtime LTS (Private) – Upgrade, Pinning, and Rollback Guidance
preview:
## Overview
This page describes how to adopt the **Runtime LTS channel** in **Redwood Private** deployments, with an emphasis on:
- **Version pinning** to a specific LTS patch (e.g., `1.18.1-lts`) for change control
- **Upgrade sequencing** that minimizes downtime and avoids mixed-version hazards
- **Rollback proced...
text:
## Overview
This page describes how to adopt the **Runtime LTS channel** in **Redwood Private** deployments, with an emphasis on:
- **Version pinning** to a specific LTS patch (e.g., `1.18.1-lts`) for change control
- **Upgrade sequencing** that minimizes downtime and avoids mixed-version hazards
- **Rollback procedures** (fast rollback to last-known-good)
- **Compatibility guidance** (installer + Helm + Kubernetes + GPU stack)
- A **post-upgrade validation checklist** suitable for regulated environments

This guidance applies to:
- Private deployments installed via the **Redwood Private Installer**
- Private deployments managed via **Helm** (including GitOps workflows)

## What LTS means in Private
Runtime LTS provides a stable runtime baseline supported for **6–12+ months** with:
- Monthly patch cadence (typical)
- Out-of-band patches for security issues when required
- Backports li...

title: On‑prem Offline Backup/Restore Bundle: Layout, Signing, and Compatibility
preview:

This document defines the **offline (air‑gapped) distribution bundle** for Redwood Private Day‑2 operations tooling related to **backup/restore** and **disaster recovery (DR)**. It standardizes:

- The **deterministic bundle layout** (so customers can mirror and verify it consistently)

text:
## Purpose
This document defines the **offline (air‑gapped) distribution bundle** for Redwood Private Day‑2 operations tooling related to **backup/restore** and **disaster recovery (DR)**. It standardizes:

- The **deterministic bundle layout** (so customers can mirror and verify it consistently)
- The **signing and checksum verification** process (supply-chain integrity)
- **Version/compatibility rules** between:
 - the bundle
 - the private installer CLI
 - the Redwood Private platform version
 - the backup artifact format

This is intended for **on‑prem and restricted egress** environments where the operator cannot fetch dependencies at runtime.

---

## Audience
- Release Engineering (bundle production + signing)
- Security Engineering (key management expectations)
- Solutions Engineering / Professional Services (customer enablement)
- Customer operators (verification steps in sec...

title: DR Plan: Redwood Private (VPC, Single-Region) — Restore into a Clean Cluster
preview:
## Purpose
This page defines the disaster recovery (DR) procedure for **Redwood Private deployed in a single cloud region inside a customer VPC**, where the recovery strategy is to **restore the Redwood control plane into a clean Kubernetes cluster in the same region** using the most recent validated backup artifact...
text:
## Purpose
This page defines the disaster recovery (DR) procedure for **Redwood Private deployed in a single cloud region inside a customer VPC**, where the recovery strategy is to **restore the Redwood control plane into a clean Kubernetes cluster in the same region** using the most recent validated backup artifacts.

This plan is intended for:
- Customer operators running Redwood Private in their VPC, and
- Redwood SRE / Solutions Engineering supporting an enterprise incident.

## Scope
### In scope
- Restore of **Redwood Private control plane state** into a new/clean cluster.
- Re-deploy of platform components (Helm/Terraform manifests) with deterministic configuration.
- DNS and TLS cutover steps (including certificate considerations).
- Post-restore validation checklist for readiness.

### Out of scope (handled elsewhere)
- Cross-region DR (see multi-region plans).
- Application-...

title: Redwood Private Day-2 Ops: Backup/Restore Toolkit Overview
preview:
## Purpose
This page provides a high-level overview of Redwood Private’s Day-2 backup/restore and disaster recovery (DR) toolkit. It is intended for:
- Customer platform teams operating Redwood Private (VPC or on-prem)
- Redwood Solutions Engineering (SE) during installation/enablement
- Redwood SRE during DR rehear...
text:
## Purpose
This page provides a high-level overview of Redwood Private’s Day-2 backup/restore and disaster recovery (DR) toolkit. It is intended for:
- Customer platform teams operating Redwood Private (VPC or on-prem)
- Redwood Solutions Engineering (SE) during installation/enablement
- Redwood SRE during DR rehearsals, restore drills, and incident response

The toolkit is designed to be:
- **Repeatable:** deterministic restore steps with documented prerequisites and ordering
- **Secure by default:** encrypted backup artifacts; no plaintext sensitive material in backup bundles
- **Operationally verifiable:** built-in verification steps and post-restore validation hooks
- **Compatible with constrained environments:** air-gapped installs and restricted IAM / limited egress

## Supported deployment types
The toolkit supports the following Redwood Private archetypes:

**VPC deployments (...

title: DR Plan (On-Prem / Air-Gapped) — Redwood Private Backup & Restore
preview:
## Purpose
This page defines the disaster recovery (DR) procedure for **Redwood Private** deployments in **on-prem / air-gapped** environments (no outbound Internet and often restricted internal networking). It focuses on:
- Offline artifact handling (installer + charts + images + docs)
- Signed bundle expectations ...
text:
## Purpose
This page defines the disaster recovery (DR) procedure for **Redwood Private** deployments in **on-prem / air-gapped** environments (no outbound Internet and often restricted internal networking). It focuses on:
- Offline artifact handling (installer + charts + images + docs)
- Signed bundle expectations and verification steps
- Restore flow that does **not** require external dependencies (no external registries, no external KMS calls, no external DNS automation)

This is an **operational plan** intended for customer platform teams and Redwood Solutions/SRE when engaged under a support contract.

## Scope
### In scope
- Restoration of **Redwood Private control plane** state from a previously created backup artifact (manifest + encrypted payload)
- Rehydrating required Kubernetes resources (CRDs/charts) from an offline bundle
- Restore validation (smoke tests + key configura...
